category_title: Children who hardly speak or do not speak at all
student_description: This student has severe expressive language impairments, relying on a vocabulary of fewer than 50 words and one-word sentences despite possessing complex cognitive concepts. They initially prefer verbal communication but become stuck when trying to express complex ideas, uniquely requiring Sign-supported Dutch (SSD) or similar multimodal communication to bridge the gap between their cognitive understanding and expressive capacity.

criteria.rationale: Because the student has complex cognitive concepts but limited verbal skills, pairing spoken words with gestures or signs provides an immediate, accessible model for alternative expression.
criteria.check: The tutor's response must explicitly describe the use of gestures, hand movements, or signs alongside their spoken dialogue.

criteria.rationale: The student initially prefers verbal communication despite its limits, so they need explicit prompts to use topic-related signs to express the complex ideas they already possess cognitively.
criteria.check: The tutor must explicitly model, offer, or prompt the student to use a specific topic-related sign or gesture to expand on their limited verbal utterance.
